dilationW 1\n#define strideT 1\n#define strideH 1\n#define strideW 1\n\ntemplate<typename T>\nconstexpr T div_up(T a, T b) {\n    return (a + b - 1) / b;\n}\n\nconstexpr int LDS_SIZE = 32*1024; 32;\n\nstatic_assert(PaddingD == 0);\nusing int32x4_t = __attribute__((__vector_size__(4 * sizeof(int)))) int;\nusing as3_uint32_ptr = __attribute__((address_space(3))) uint32_t *;\n\ntemplate<uint16_t cnt>\n__device__ __inline__ void s_waitcnt_lgkmcnt() {\n    asm volatile (\"s_waitcnt lgkmcnt(%0)\\n\"::\"i\"(cnt));\n}\n\ntemplate<uint16_t cnt>\n__device__ __inline__ void as3_uint32_ptr vaddr = (as3_uint32_ptr)(psrc);\n    asm volatile(\"ds_read_b32 %[vdst], %[vaddr] offset:%[offset]\"\n                : [vdst]\"=v\"((uint32_t&)(v))\n                : < KD; d ++) {\n                #pragma unroll\n                for(int h = 0; h < KH; h++) {\n                    #pragma unroll\n                    for(int w = 0; w < KW; w+=2) {\n Keep only one kernel row of\n    // LDS data live at a time instead of materializing the whole 3-D window.\n    // The three packed reads below contain the six consecutive input values\n    // needed by both outputs for one (d,h) row.\n    for (int oi = KW_PACK*threadIdx.x; oi < num_outputs; oi += KW_PACK*blockDim.x) {\n        const int oh = oi / BLOCK_W;\n        const int ow = oi % BLOCK_W;\n\n        const int ih = oh;\n        const int iw = ow;\n\n        const int srci0 = (ih) * padded_W + (iw);\n        float sum0 = bias_value;\n        float sum1 = bias_value;\n\n        #pragma unroll\n        for(int d = 0; d < KD; d ++) {\n            #pragma unroll\n            for(int h = 0; h < KH; h += 3) {\n                IO_DTYPE row_input[3][KWR][KW_PACK];\n                #pragma unroll\n                for (int hh = 0; hh < 3; ++hh) {\n                    if (h + hh < KH) {\n                        #pragma unroll\n                        for(int w = 0; w < KW; w+=KW_PACK) {\n                            int src_off = d*(padded_H * padded_W) + (PaddingH + h + hh - KH/2)*padded_W + (PaddingW + w - KW/2);\n                            reinterpret_cast<uint32_t&>(row_input[hh][w/KW_PACK]) = ds_read_b32(s_input + srci0, src_off*sizeof(IO_DTYPE));\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                s_waitcnt_lgkmcnt<0>();\n                __builtin_amdgcn_sched_barrier(0);\n\n                #pragma unroll\n                for (int hh = 0; hh < 3; ++hh) {\n                    if (h + hh < KH) {\n                        #pragma unroll\n                        for(int w = 0; w < KW; w+=2) {\n                            v_fmac_f32(sum0, weight_reg[d][h+hh][w+0], (float)(row_input[hh][w/KW_PACK][0]));\n                            v_fmac_f32(sum1, weight_reg[d][h+hh][w+0], (float)(row_input[hh][w/KW_PACK][1]));\n                            if (w+1 < KW) {\n                                v_fmac_f32(sum0, weight_reg[d][h+hh][w+1], (float)(row_input[hh][w/KW_PACK][1]));\n                                v_fmac_f32(sum1, weight_reg[d][h+hh][w+1], (float)(row_input[hh][w/KW_PACK+1][0]));\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        output[oh * oW + ow + 0] = (IO_DTYPE)(sum0);\n        output[oh * oW + ow + 1] = (IO_DTYPE)(sum1);\n    }\n}\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

This value represents the number of waves that are sent to each SQ. This only counts new waves sent since the start of collection (for dispatch profiling this is the timeframe of kernel execution, for agent profiling it is the timeframe between start_context and read counter data). A sum of all SQ_WAVES values will give the total number of waves started by the application during the collection timeframe. Falls slightly short of total request latency because some fetches are divided into two requests that may finish at different times and this counter collects the average latency of the two. This value represents the number of waves that are sent to each SQ. This only counts new waves sent since the start of collection (for dispatch profiling this is the timeframe of kernel execution, for agent profiling it is the timeframe between start_context and read counter data). A sum of all SQ_WAVES values will give the total number of waves started by the application during the collection timeframe. Returns one value per-SE (aggregates of SIMD values).\nBlock               :\tSQ\nDimensions          :\tDIMENSION_INSTANCE[0:0] DIMENSION_SHADER_ENGINE[0:3] DIMENSION_XCC[0:7]\n\n\nCounter_Name        :\tSQ_WAVES_EQ_64\nDescription         :\tCount number of waves with exactly 64 active threads sent to SQs.
